maxContractTerm: **5 years, with the Commissioner of Education authorized to grant one one-year extension beyond the 5-year term (maximum effective ceiling = 6 years).** Maine Revised Statutes Title 20-A, §5401(13)(A) provides verbatim: "A contract may not exceed a period of 5 years, except that the commissioner may authorize a one-year extension of a 5-year contract when such an extension would be beneficial to a school administrative unit." [PL 2003, c. 325, §1 (AMD).] Re-verified on the official Maine Legislature section page (https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/20-a/title20-asec5401.html) on 2026-06-23. Under §5401(13)(B), any contract "existing beyond one year" must be authorized by the school administrative unit's governing body, and under §5401(13)(C), in a school administrative district the superintendent must execute the contract with the approval of the board of directors. The statute caps a single award; it does not bar a fresh competitive re-award to the same vendor following a new §5402 bid.
rebidRequired: **Conditional — competitive sealed bidding under 20-A M.R.S. §5402 is required for transportation contracts above $4,000 that are between a school administrative unit and a private contractor; contracts between school administrative units (SAU-to-SAU) and bus-service contracts under $4,000 are exempt.** §5401(13)(D) provides that any transportation contract "shall be submitted for bid in conformity with the procedures outlined in section 5402." §5402 prescribes mandatory written sealed-bid procedure (sealed envelopes marked "Bid, not to be opened until ..."; sealed-bid filing with the superintendent; no opening before the appointed time; public opening; public examination/read-aloud if citizens or press are present). At term expiration, because §5401(13)(A) caps a single award and a successor agreement is a new "contract" subject to §5401(13)(D), the §5402 sealed-bid process applies to any new contract — there is no statutory authority for a non-competitive renewal beyond the single commissioner-authorized one-year extension of an executed 5-year contract. The §5402(5) exceptions are limited to: "(A) Contracts for bus services under $4,000" and "(B) Contracts between school administration units." The statute does NOT provide sole-source, emergency-procurement, cooperative-purchasing/piggyback, or board-waiver exceptions for private-contractor pupil-transportation contracts. Re-verified on https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/20-a/title20-Asec5402.html on 2026-06-23.
renewalMechanics: The only statutorily authorized mechanism for continuing a transportation contract beyond its original awarded term without a new competitive bid is the **single one-year commissioner-authorized extension of an executed 5-year contract** under §5401(13)(A): "the commissioner may authorize a one-year extension of a 5-year contract when such an extension would be beneficial to a school administrative unit." The extension is discretionary with the Commissioner of Education, must be sought by the SAU, and is limited to a single one-year extension. §5401(13)(B) additionally requires the SAU's governing body to authorize any contract existing beyond one year, which by its terms also reaches the extended contract. The statute contains **no other renewal mechanisms** — no automatic rollover, no evergreen renewal, no CPI-indexed adjustment authority, no fuel-escalator authority, no mutual-written-agreement renewal authority, and no provision for successive renewal periods beyond the single one-year commissioner extension. After the 5-year + 1-year commissioner extension is exhausted, the SAU must re-bid under §5402 to procure transportation services (whether from the same or a new vendor). The statute does not address mid-contract amendments (e.g., price escalators), leaving those to the negotiated contract terms within the term ceiling.
statutoryCitation: **Primary authority — transportation-specific statute (Tier 1 codified statute):** 20-A M.R.S. §5401(13)(A)–(D) (Maximum term; governing-body and board authorization; sealed-bid cross-reference) and 20-A M.R.S. §5402 (Bid procedure, with §5402(5) exceptions for contracts under $4,000 and between SAUs). Most recent substantive amendment to §5401(13)(A): PL 2003, c. 325, §1. §5402 has not been amended since its enactment in PL 1983, c. 175, §2. Official source URLs: section page https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/20-a/title20-asec5401.html; §5402 page https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/20-a/title20-Asec5402.html; chapter PDF https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/20-a/title20-Ach215.pdf (Revisor's PDF generated 10.20.2025; current through October 1, 2025 reflecting the First Special Session of the 132nd Maine Legislature). Chapter 215 index (https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/20-a/title20-Ach215sec0.html) confirms the chapter contains only §5401 and §5402. **Supplementary authority (Tier 2 administrative regulation):** 05-071 C.M.R. Chapter 81 (School Transportation Safety), Section 8 ("Contracts for School Transportation Services"), repealed-and-replaced effective September 16, 2017; statutory authority cited in the rule is 29-A M.R.S. §2311 and 20-A M.R.S. §5401(17); official PDF at https://www.maine.gov/doe/sites/maine.gov.doe/files/inline-files/School%20Safety%20Transportation%20-%20Chapter%2081%20-%203.26.2025_0.pdf. **General procurement statutes (checked and confirmed inapplicable to SAU transportation contracts):** Title 5 M.R.S. §1825-B applies only to "the State or any department or agency of the State" — re-verified scope language on 2026-06-23; Title 20-A M.R.S. §1314 is a parallel SAD bid-procedure provision (sealed envelope; no early opening; public opening; examination/read-aloud) that imposes no transportation-specific term limit and is not the controlling procedure for transportation (the controlling procedure is §5402, expressly cross-referenced from §5401(13)(D)); §1314(5) (Exceptions) is REPEALED.
districtVariance: **Unknown.** No Maine Attorney General opinion, Department of Education guidance memo, state-auditor report, or Maine School Boards Association handbook section was located that addresses whether SAUs interpret §5401(13) or §5402 differently from one another. The statute is textually unambiguous on the 5-year + 1-year commissioner-extension ceiling and the mandatory sealed-bid procedure, leaving little interpretive surface area, and the DOE Transportation Laws page (https://www.maine.gov/doe/schools/transportation/laws) publishes no separate guidance memo, manual, or contract template that would displace the §5401(13)/§5402 framework. However, no source affirmatively confirms uniform statewide application across all Maine SAUs. Searches of MSBA handbook references, DOE transportation pages, and Maine AG opinion archives did not surface a documented split. The verdict is therefore Unknown rather than Yes or No.
specialEducation: **Maine law sets NO different contract term limit, rebid requirement, procurement rule, or renewal mechanic for special-education transportation contracts.** Title 20-A §5401(4) provides: "Special education students shall be provided transportation: A. As provided by chapter 301; or B. To and from classes." Chapter 303 (Children with Disabilities) §7204(1) designates transportation as a "related service" the commissioner must cause to be provided ("Shall provide, or cause to be provided by administrative units operating schools, all related services, as defined in rules the commissioner establishes, required by a child with a disability so that the child may benefit from equal educational opportunities"), and §7251-A authorizes SAUs to "offer or contract for special education services" and "offer or contract for related services in accordance with rules adopted by the department pursuant to section 7005." Re-verified §7251-A and §7204(1) on official Maine Legislature pages on 2026-06-23. Chapter 303 contains **no separate contract-term ceiling, no separate rebid requirement, and no separate renewal mechanic** for special-education or IEP-mandated transportation vendors. The general §5401(13) 5-year + 1-year-extension ceiling and the §5402 sealed-bid procedure (with the §5402(5)(A) $4,000 small-purchase exception and §5402(5)(B) inter-SAU exemption) accordingly apply to special-education transportation contracts the same as to regular-route contracts. Federal IDEA (20 U.S.C. §1400 et seq.) and McKinney-Vento (42 U.S.C. §11431 et seq.) requirements impose duties to transport but do not alter state contract or procurement rules. No separate provisions for McKinney-Vento, foster-care, alternative student transportation, or disability-vendor contracts were located in Maine statute or DOE rule.
notesAndSpecialProvisions:
- **Small-purchase exemption from sealed bidding.** 20-A M.R.S. §5402(5)(A) exempts "Contracts for bus services under $4,000" from the sealed-bid procedure. The $4,000 threshold has not been updated since enactment in PL 1983, c. 175 and is rarely operative for full-route service contracts.
- **Inter-school-unit exemption.** 20-A M.R.S. §5402(5)(B) exempts "Contracts between school administration units" from the sealed-bid requirement, providing the only statutory authority for SAU-to-SAU shared-transportation arrangements without bidding. Combined with §5401(2)(B) (SAD secondary-school-privileges authority) and §5401(3)(B) (community school district committees' written-agreement authority), this creates a limited inter-governmental alternative to private competitive procurement.
- **Sole-source, emergency, cooperative-purchasing, piggyback authority — not provided for private-contractor service contracts.** The transportation statute contains no sole-source, emergency-procurement, cooperative-purchasing, interlocal/piggyback, or board-waiver authority specific to SAU-to-private-contractor pupil-transportation contracts. State-level cooperative purchasing under Title 5 does not extend to SAU transportation contracts (Title 5 §1825-B reaches only state agencies).
- **Education service centers (Chapter 123) — alternative non-private-contractor pathway.** 20-A M.R.S. Chapter 123 authorizes SAU members of an Education Service Center to purchase services from the center, including "Transportation, transportation routing and vehicle maintenance" as a Category 4 facilities-and-transportation service. This is an inter-governmental services pathway (SAU member purchasing from an Education Service Center), not a substitute for the §5402 sealed-bid requirement when an SAU contracts with a private contractor.
- **State-board cooperative-recommendation framework (§405).** 20-A M.R.S. §405 authorizes the state board to recommend that SAUs develop cooperative agreements that "may include, but are not limited to: purchasing or contract agreements," but this is a state-board recommendation framework — not direct statutory authority to bypass §5402 for transportation services from a private contractor.
- **Commissioner's role.** The commissioner's authority over private-contractor transportation contracts is limited to (i) authorizing the one-year extension under §5401(13)(A) and (ii) approving school-bus purchases, contracts and leases (i.e., the bus-acquisition side) under §5401(15) (separate $5,000,000 annual cap on purchase approvals and 5-year maximum loan/lease-purchase term, extended to 15 years for zero-emission buses by PL 2021, c. 693, §2). The commissioner does not pre-approve every transportation service contract; under 05-071 C.M.R. Chapter 81 §8, SAUs must present the contract for DOE review "upon request" along with a §5402 compliance statement.
- **Governing-body authorization.** §5401(13)(B) — any transportation contract "existing beyond one year" requires authorization by the SAU's governing body. In a SAD, §5401(13)(C) requires the superintendent to execute the contract with the approval of the board of directors. These are concurrent requirements: a multi-year contract requires both the §5402 sealed-bid process and §5401(13)(B)/(C) board/governing-body authorization.
- **Award standard.** §5402 prescribes sealed-bid mechanics but does not codify a "lowest responsible bidder" award standard. School law commentary (e.g., Drummond Woodsum School Law Advisory 267, citing 20-A M.R.S. §5401(13)(D) and §5402) describes the customary practice as award to the lowest responsible bidder with discretion to reject non-conforming bids, but this is practice/guidance rather than a statutory mandate.
- **Bus acquisition vs. service contracts.** The 5-year ceiling in §5401(13)(A) governs service contracts; a parallel 5-year ceiling applies to loans and lease-purchase agreements for bus acquisition under §5401(15)(C), extended to 15 years for zero-emission school buses (PL 2021, c. 693, §2).
- **Special-education transportation.** No separate procurement framework; general §5401(13)/§5402 controls. §7204(1) treats transportation as a "related service" the commissioner must cause to be provided; §7251-A permits SAUs to contract for "special education services" and "related services." See specialEducation field.
- **Charter schools — exempt from SAU statutes including §5401/§5402.** Title 20-A §2412 provides: "Except as provided in this chapter and its charter contract, a public charter school is exempt from all statutes and rules applicable to a noncharter public school, a local school board or a school administrative unit." Charter schools must "have a plan that describes how the school will provide transportation" but are not bound by the §5401(13) term cap or the §5402 sealed-bid procedure for their own transportation procurement. Civil rights, health/safety, assessment, conflict-of-interest, public records, criminal-history-checks, and special-education requirements remain non-exemptable.
- **DOE administrative rule.** 05-071 C.M.R. Chapter 81 §8 ("Contracts for School Transportation Services") requires SAUs to present transportation/lease contracts to DOE for review upon request along with a §5402 compliance statement; statutory authority is 29-A M.R.S. §2311 and 20-A M.R.S. §5401(17); repealed and replaced effective September 16, 2017.
- **State-level Maine School Bus Bid Module.** Maine DOE / Division of Procurement Services jointly operate a statewide Invitation-to-Bid module for school bus *vehicle purchases* (https://www.maine.gov/doe/schools/transportation/programs/busbidmodule) — this is a vehicle-procurement vehicle, not a service-contract vehicle, and does not displace §5402 for SAU-to-private-contractor service contracts.
- **General municipal/SAD contracting statutes.** Title 30-A M.R.S. §124 (municipal bids/awards) and Title 20-A §1314 (SAD bid procedure) are parallel provisions but the controlling, transportation-specific statute is §5402, expressly cross-referenced from §5401(13)(D). §1314(5) (Exceptions) is REPEALED — confirmed on 2026-06-23 via the official §1314 page. Title 5 M.R.S. §1825-B (state procurement) applies only to state agencies and does not reach SAUs.
exceptionsUnresolved:
- **District-Level Interpretation Variance** — recorded as Unknown. Searches of MSBA handbook references, DOE transportation guidance pages, and Maine AG opinion archives did not surface either (a) affirmative evidence of uniform statewide application or (b) evidence of differing SAU interpretations. The statute's textual clarity (5-year + one commissioner-authorized extension; mandatory §5402 sealed-bid with two narrow exceptions) makes a documented interpretive split unlikely, but absence of an affirmative DOE or AG uniformity opinion prevents a confident "No." Negative search path: queries on Maine School Boards Association/MSMA handbook ("transportation contract bidding"); DOE transportation laws and policies page (https://www.maine.gov/doe/schools/transportation/laws — confirmed it publishes no separate term/rebid/renewal guidance); Maine AG opinions on transportation contracts (Maine AG opinion database — none specifically construing §5401(13) or §5402 surfaced); the MSBA Handbook PDF returned HTTP 403 in Stage 1 and could not be retrieved.

All five required fields (Max Contract Term, Rebid Required, Renewal Mechanics, Statutory Citation, District Variance) are answered on primary authority; the only field carrying an "Unknown" verdict is District Variance, which is documented as such with the negative search path above.

sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: §5401(13)(A): 'A contract may not exceed a period of 5 years, except that the commissioner may authorize a one-year extension of a 5-year contract when such an extension would be beneficial to a school administrative unit.' [PL 2003, c. 325, §1 (AMD).]  §5401(13)(B): 'Authorization by the unit's governing body shall be required for contracts existing beyond one year.'  §5401(13)(C): 'In a school administrative district, the superintendent shall execute the contract with the approval of the board of directors.'  §5401(13)(D): 'The contract shall be submitted for bid in conformity with the procedures outlined in section 5402.'  §5401(4): 'Special education students shall be provided transportation: A. As provided by chapter 301; or B. To and from classes.'

Chapter index confirms only §§5401 and 5402 in the chapter (https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/20-a/title20-Ach215sec0.html).

sourceLedgerRows.codeCurrency: Current through October 1, 2025; original enactment PL 1983, c. 175, §2; no subsequent amendments
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics (because successor contract triggers fresh §5402 bid); Statutory Citation
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: §5402(1): Written sealed bids filed with superintendent, sealed envelope marked 'Bid, not to be opened until ...'. §5402(2): No bid opening before appointed time. §5402(3): Public opening by superintendent (or designated board member). §5402(4): Bids made available for examination or read aloud if citizens or press present. §5402(5): 'The following contracts are exempt from the requirements of this section: A. Contracts for bus services under $4,000; and B. Contracts between school administration units.'

A school administrative unit may also offer or contract for related services in accordance with rules adopted by the department pursuant to section 7005.' sourceLedgerRows.sourceTier: Tier 1 — General procurement statute (checked and confirmed inapplicable to SAUs)
sourceLedgerRows.fieldsSupported: Confirms that the general state procurement statute does NOT reach school administrative units (limited to 'the State or any department or agency of the State'), so the controlling authority for SAU transportation contracts is exclusively Title 20-A §§5401–5402
sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: Operative scope language: 'The Director of the Bureau of General Services shall purchase collectively all goods and services for the State or any department or agency of the State.' No language extends §1825-B to SAUs, SADs, boards of education, or local political subdivisions.

sourceLedgerRows.sourceTier: Tier 1 — Codified state statute (parallel SAD bid-procedure provision; not controlling for transportation)

sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: §1314 mirrors §5402's sealed-bid mechanics for SADs generally (sealed-envelope filing; no early opening; public opening by superintendent/designated director; citizen/press examination or read-aloud). Subsection 5 (Exceptions) is repealed. Section does not specify covered contract types, term limits, or renewal procedures.

sourceLedgerRows.relevantText: 'Except as provided in this chapter and its charter contract, a public charter school is exempt from all statutes and rules applicable to a noncharter public school, a local school board or a school administrative unit.' Non-exemptable categories include civil rights, health/safety, assessment, conflict-of-interest, public records, criminal-history checks, and special-education requirements. Charter schools must have a transportation plan but are not bound by the §5401(13) term cap or §5402 sealed bid for their own procurement.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term — 5 years + 1-year commissioner extension (6-year ceiling)
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Longer-term authority exists somewhere in Maine law (e.g., 'notwithstanding,' multi-year option, successive renewal, grandfathered contracts, or a parallel statute permitting >5 years)
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened §5401 on official Maine Legislature page; re-searched §5401(13) for 'notwithstanding'/'may renew'/'extension'/'option'/'successive'/'multi-year'/'not exceed'/'annual termination'; searched 132nd Legislature bill directory for §5401 amendments 2024–2026; checked §5401(15)/(15-A) (which apply only to bus acquisition, not service contracts); checked Chapter 215 chapter index (§5401 and §5402 are the only sections); checked §1314 (no transportation term limit); checked Title 5 §1825-B (state agencies only)
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed — §5401(13)(A) is the controlling cap; no longer-term statute, grandfather clause, or successive-renewal authority exists. The only commissioner extension is a single one-year extension. Recent §5401 amendments (PL 2021, c. 693, §2) touched §5401(15)/(15-A) bus-acquisition provisions, not §5401(13). §5401(13)(A) has not been amended since PL 2003, c. 325.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Rebid Required — Conditional (sealed bid required for >$4,000 SAU-private contracts; SAU-to-SAU and <$4,000 exempt)
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Sole-source, emergency-procurement, cooperative-purchasing/piggyback, board-waiver, or negotiated-procurement exceptions exist that the pack missed
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened §5402 on official page; verified the §5402(5) exception list is exhaustive (A=under $4,000; B=between SAUs); searched 'cooperative purchasing'/'piggyback'/'sole source'/'emergency procurement'/'negotiated procurement'/'board waiver' against Title 20-A and the DOE Transportation Laws page; checked Title 5 §1825-B (state agencies only); checked §405 (state-board recommendation framework, not direct authority); checked Chapter 123 Education Service Centers (alternative inter-governmental pathway, not §5402 substitute); searched DOE for any waiver/exception guidance memo
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed — the §5402(5) two-item exception list is exhaustive for sealed-bid carve-outs. No sole-source, emergency, cooperative-purchasing/piggyback, or board-waiver authority exists for SAU-to-private-contractor pupil-transportation contracts. Charter schools (under §2412) and Education Service Centers (Chapter 123) operate outside this framework but are not exceptions WITHIN §5402.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics — only commissioner one-year extension; no CPI, fuel-escalator, automatic rollover, or mutual-agreement renewal
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Statute or DOE rule authorizes CPI/index adjustments, fuel escalators, automatic rollover, multiple one-year extensions, mutual-written-agreement renewal, or board-approved renewal without rebid
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-searched §5401(13) and §5402 for 'CPI'/'index'/'fuel'/'rollover'/'evergreen'/'automatic renewal'/'mutual'/'agreement'/'extension'/'option'; checked 05-071 CMR Chapter 81 §8 (incorporates §5402 by reference, no separate renewal authority); checked DOE Transportation Laws page (no renewal guidance memo); checked §5401(15)/(15-A) (bus-acquisition lease/loan terms — separate framework with its own 5-year/15-year ceilings)
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed — the commissioner one-year extension under §5401(13)(A) is the sole renewal mechanism without a new §5402 bid. The statute is silent on mid-contract amendments (e.g., price escalators), leaving those to the negotiated contract terms.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Statutory Citation — §5401(13)(A)-(D) and §5402; Chapter 81 §8 supplementary; Title 5 §1825-B inapplicable
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Cited sections are misnumbered, repealed, superseded, recently amended, or the actual controlling statute is something else (e.g., Title 30-A §124 for municipal SAUs; §1314 for SADs as a transportation-specific procedure)
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened each cited section on official Maine Legislature pages; confirmed Chapter 215 index lists only §§5401 and 5402; confirmed §1314 is parallel SAD bid-procedure (not transportation-specific) and §1314(5) Exceptions is REPEALED; confirmed Title 5 §1825-B scope is limited to state agencies; checked 132nd Legislature bill directory and 2025 Public Laws (e.g., 2025 PL c. 362) for §5401/§5402 amendments — none altering §5401(13) or §5402; checked code-currency disclaimer (Generated 10.20.2025; current through October 1, 2025)
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed — citations re-verified verbatim on official Maine Legislature pages. §5401(13)(A)-(D) and §5402 are the controlling statutes; §1314 is parallel and non-controlling for transportation; Title 5 §1825-B does not reach SAUs; Chapter 81 §8 incorporates §5402 by reference.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Special Education — same §5401(13) cap and §5402 sealed bid; no separate special-ed procurement framework
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Chapter 303 (Children with Disabilities) or a DOE special-education rule sets a separate contract-term, rebid, or renewal rule for IEP/special-education/McKinney-Vento/foster-care transportation vendors
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Re-opened §7251-A and §7204(1) on official Maine Legislature pages; reviewed the special-education chapter (Chapter 303) for any procurement-specific provision; searched DOE special-education rules and guidance for separate procurement framework; searched 'McKinney-Vento'/'foster care'/'homeless student'/'alternative transportation' contract procurement provisions in Maine statute and rule
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed — Chapter 303 (§7251-A, §7204(1)) treats transportation as a 'related service' SAUs may offer or contract for, but contains no separate contract-term, rebid, or renewal rule. §5401(13)(A) and §5402 control. No McKinney-Vento, foster-care, or alternative-student transportation procurement provision locates in Maine statute or DOE rule. Federal IDEA and McKinney-Vento create duties to transport but do not alter state contracting rules.

challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: A DOE memo, Maine AG opinion, MSBA handbook section, or state-auditor report exists that affirmatively documents either uniform statewide application or a documented split among SAUs interpreting §5401(13)/§5402 differently
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Searched Maine AG opinions database for §5401/§5402 interpretive opinions; searched DOE Transportation Laws and Policies pages for uniformity memo; searched MSBA/MSMA handbook references for transportation contract bidding section; reviewed Drummond Woodsum School Law Advisory 267 (pathfinder only)
challengeMatrixRows.result: Unresolved — no affirmative DOE/AG uniformity opinion located, and no documented split located. Recorded as Unknown per the brief's verdict ladder (sourced credible counter-evidence does not exist on either side). The textual unambiguity of the statute makes a documented split unlikely but cannot be ruled out without an exhaustive review of all ~200 SAU procurement manuals.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: Confidence on this single field reduced to Medium-Low; overall pack confidence remains High because the four substantive fields are fully resolved on primary authority

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Currentness of cited statutes (have §5401(13) or §5402 been amended, repealed, renumbered, or superseded after the Oct 1, 2025 code-currency date?)
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: A 132nd Legislature, Second Regular Session bill amends or repeals §5401(13) or §5402 after Oct 1, 2025; OR a pending effective-date amendment alters the term cap or sealed-bid procedure
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Searched 132nd Maine Legislature bill directory for 2025–2026 bills referencing §5401 or §5402; checked Maine DOE 132nd-Legislature Education Bills Summary (07.2025); searched 2025 Public Laws for amendments to Title 20-A Chapter 215; checked Office of the Revisor of Statutes generation timestamp (10.20.2025); cross-checked LD numbers for school transportation bills
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed — no post-currency amendment located that affects §5401(13) or §5402. Recent legislative activity on school transportation focuses on bus speed limits (LD 312) and zero-emission fleet goals (§5401(15)/(15-A)), not §5401(13) or §5402.

challengeMatrixRows.findingChallenged: Applicability boundary — does the §5401/§5402 framework reach every K-12 public-school transportation contract in Maine, or are there carve-outs (charter schools, education service centers, federal-program transportation, regional cooperatives)?
challengeMatrixRows.disconfirmingHypothesis: Charter schools, ESC member SAUs, federally-funded transportation programs, or other carve-outs operate under different procurement rules that should be flagged as a scope qualifier on the main pack
challengeMatrixRows.challengeSearches: Checked §2412 (charter-school general exemption); checked Chapter 123 Education Service Centers (alternative pathway); checked §405 (state-board cooperative-agreement recommendation framework); confirmed Chapter 113-A Regional Education Cooperatives is REPEALED
challengeMatrixRows.result: Confirmed with refinement — §5401(13)/§5402 controls SAU-to-private-contractor pupil-transportation service contracts. Charter schools are exempt from SAU statutes under §2412 (added to Notes). ESC member SAUs may purchase transportation services from an ESC under Chapter 123 (added to Notes — this is an inter-governmental pathway, not a §5402 substitute when contracting with a private contractor). Chapter 113-A is repealed — confirmed.
challengeMatrixRows.confidenceImpact: High — pack refined with charter-school and ESC notes; no change to the substantive five-field findings

perFieldVerdicts.note: §5401(13)(A) re-opened on official page; verbatim text confirms 5 years + single commissioner one-year extension; no longer-term or successive-renewal authority surfaces; no post-Oct-1-2025 amendment located.

§5402 re-opened on official page; §5402(5)(A)-(B) exception list confirmed exhaustive for sealed-bid carve-outs; no sole-source/emergency/cooperative/piggyback/waiver authority exists for SAU-to-private-contractor pupil-transportation contracts.

§5401(13)(A) commissioner one-year extension is the sole authority to continue beyond the original 5-year term without a fresh §5402 bid. No CPI, fuel-escalator, rollover, evergreen, mutual-agreement, or successive renewal authority surfaces in statute or DOE rule.

All cited sections re-verified verbatim on official Maine Legislature pages on 2026-06-23. Chapter 215 index confirms only §§5401 and 5402 in the transportation chapter. Title 5 §1825-B scope is limited to state agencies. §1314 is parallel SAD procedure (and §1314(5) Exceptions is REPEALED), non-controlling for transportation.

Recorded as Unknown — no affirmative DOE/AG uniformity opinion located, and no documented split located. The textual unambiguity of §5401(13) and §5402 makes a documented interpretive split unlikely, but absence of an authoritative uniformity opinion prevents a confident 'No.' Negative search path documented in exceptionsUnresolved.

Chapter 303 §§7204(1) and 7251-A re-verified on official pages. Transportation is a 'related service' SAUs may contract for, but Chapter 303 contains no separate contract-term, rebid, or renewal rule. §5401(13)/§5402 controls. No McKinney-Vento, foster-care, or alternative-student transportation procurement provision located.

verificationNotes:
**Search strategy.** Stage 2 re-opened each official-source URL from the Stage 1 pack and re-read the underlying statutes; ran disconfirming searches on each field per the brief's challenge patterns; checked the 132nd Maine Legislature (2025–2026) bill directory and the DOE Education-Bills Summary for any post-Oct-1-2025 amendment to §5401(13) or §5402; verified the chapter index confirms Chapter 215 contains only §§5401 and 5402; verified §2412 charter-school exemption and §405 cooperative-agreement framework; confirmed Chapter 113-A Regional Education Cooperatives is REPEALED.

**Queries that mattered.** 'Maine 132nd Legislature 2025 2026 20-A 5401 OR 5402 school transportation contract amendment LD'; 'Maine 20-A charter school transportation contract bidding procurement statute'; 'Maine school administrative unit cooperative purchasing OR piggyback transportation contract authority statute'; 'Maine Attorney General opinion school transportation contract bidding 20-A 5401 OR 5402'; 'Maine Chapter 215 Title 20-A 2025 amendment session law pupil transportation'; 'Maine Chapter 81 School Transportation Safety Section 8 contracts 5402 effective 2017'.

**Current-law check.** Official Maine Revisor's PDF of Chapter 215 generated 10.20.2025 and bears the disclaimer 'current through October 1, 2025' reflecting changes through the First Special Session of the 132nd Maine Legislature. No 2025–2026 session legislation locates that amends §5401(13) (contract-term provision) or §5402 (bid-procedure provision). The 132nd Legislature's school-transportation activity focused on bus speed and zero-emission fleet goals (touching §5401(15)/(15-A) bus-acquisition provisions). The most recent substantive amendment to §5401(13)(A) remains PL 2003, c. 325, §1; §5402 has not been amended since enactment in PL 1983, c. 175, §2. 05-071 C.M.R. Chapter 81 was repealed and replaced September 16, 2017 (filing 2017-139) and remains in force.

**SOURCE-VALIDATION REVISIT (load-bearing primary citations).** (1) 20-A M.R.S. §5401(13)(A) — re-opened on https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/20-a/title20-asec5401.html on 2026-06-23; verbatim text confirmed: 'A contract may not exceed a period of 5 years, except that the commissioner may authorize a one-year extension of a 5-year contract when such an extension would be beneficial to a school administrative unit.' [PL 2003, c. 325, §1 (AMD).] VERIFIED. (2) 20-A M.R.S. §5401(13)(B)-(D) — re-opened on same page; verbatim text of governing-body authorization, SAD board-of-directors approval, and §5402 cross-reference confirmed. VERIFIED. (3) 20-A M.R.S. §5402 — re-opened on https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/20-a/title20-Asec5402.html on 2026-06-23; sealed-bid procedure subsections (1)-(4) and §5402(5)(A)-(B) exceptions confirmed verbatim. VERIFIED. (4) 20-A M.R.S. §7251-A — re-opened on https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/20-A/title20-Asec7251-A.html on 2026-06-23; verbatim 'A school administrative unit may offer or contract for special education services. A school administrative unit may also offer or contract for related services in accordance with rules adopted by the department pursuant to section 7005.' VERIFIED. (5) 20-A M.R.S. §7204(1) — re-opened on https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/20-A/title20-Asec7204.html on 2026-06-23; verbatim 'related services' duty confirmed. VERIFIED. (6) 5 M.R.S. §1825-B — re-opened on https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/5/title5sec1825-B.html on 2026-06-23; operative scope language limited to 'the State or any department or agency of the State.' VERIFIED. (7) 20-A M.R.S. §1314 — re-opened on https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/20-a/title20-Asec1314.html on 2026-06-23; subsections (1)-(4) confirmed; subsection (5) Exceptions is REPEALED. VERIFIED (with correction note added to ledger). (8) 20-A M.R.S. §2412 — re-opened on https://legislature.maine.gov/legis/statutes/20-A/title20-Asec2412.html on 2026-06-23; charter-school general exemption from SAU statutes confirmed. VERIFIED (added to Notes). (9) 20-A M.R.S. §405 — re-opened on https://www.mainelegislature.org/legis/statutes/20-a/title20-Asec405.html on 2026-06-23; state-board cooperative-agreement recommendation framework confirmed (not a §5402 substitute). VERIFIED (added to Notes). (10) Chapter 215 chapter index — re-opened on https://legislature.maine.gov/legis/statutes/20-A/title20-Ach215sec0.html on 2026-06-23; confirms Chapter 215 contains only §§5401 and 5402. VERIFIED. (11) DOE Transportation Laws page — re-opened on https://www.maine.gov/doe/schools/transportation/laws on 2026-06-23; lists 20-A Chapter 215 and 05-071 CMR Chapters 81, 83, 85, 86; publishes no separate term/rebid/renewal guidance. VERIFIED. (12) 05-071 C.M.R. Chapter 81 PDF — WebFetch and firecrawl both unable to render the PDF text directly (binary/flate-compressed); chapter existence, repeal-and-replace effective date (September 16, 2017), and Section 8 contract-review-on-request requirement corroborated via DOE Newsroom announcement (https://mainedoenews.net/2017/09/18/repeal-and-replacement-of-chapter-81-uniform-school-bus-standards-for-pupil-transportation-in-maine/) and Stage 1's prior read; PDF-text re-verification is PARTIALLY VERIFIED — see note (the rule's incorporation of §5402 by reference is corroborated; the verbatim Section 8 text was not re-extracted in Stage 2).

**Negative search paths.** District Variance: Maine AG opinion database — no opinion specifically construing §5401(13) or §5402 located; MSBA Handbook S3 PDF — returned HTTP 403 in Stage 1, not re-attempted in Stage 2; DOE Transportation Laws page — confirmed publishes no variance memo. Special-education-specific contract rule: Chapter 303 reviewed — §7204(1) and §7251-A confirm 'related services' framing without separate procurement rule; no McKinney-Vento, foster-care, alternative-student, or disability-vendor contract-term provision located in Maine statute or DOE rule. Cooperative-purchasing/piggyback authority for SAU-to-private-contractor transportation: §5401 contains no such provision; §5402(5)(B) covers only SAU-to-SAU contracts; Title 5 §1825-B (state procurement) does not reach SAUs; §405 and Chapter 123 (ESCs) provide inter-governmental pathways, not §5402 substitutes. Post-Oct-1-2025 amendments: 132nd Legislature Second Regular Session bill directory checked — no bill amending §5401(13) or §5402 located.

**Lane status.** Maine lane completed Stage 2 with all five required fields plus Special Education resolved on primary authority; one field (District Variance) carries an Unknown verdict with documented negative search path. No re-research pass triggered (no challenge rejected a finding; one challenge produced refinement-only outputs added to Notes).

Contents corroborated by re-opening §5401 and §5402 HTML pages and the Chapter 215 index page.)
